perturbation.original_text: 3. The Receiving Party shall preserve Proprietary Information received from the Disclosing Party in confidence by exercising at least the same degree of care used to restrict disclosure and use of the Receiving Party's own similar confidential information, but at least a reasonable degree of care, and shall refrain from disclosing such Propriety Information to its employees not involved in the Pilot Study, its consultants, representatives, or agents ( the "Affiliates") or to any third party without written authorization from the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall be construed to bind and impose obligations upon any other divisions, subsidiaries, business units, and/or affiliated companies of the Receiving Party that receive access to the Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by its Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party will ensure that all of its Affiliates sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing confidentiality restrictions at least as restrictive as those set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Party shall preserve Proprietary Information received from the Disclosing Party in confidence and shall refrain from disclosing such Propriety Information to its employees not involved in the Pilot Study, its consultants, representatives, or agents ( the "Affiliates") or to any third party without written authorization from the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall be construed to bind and impose obligations upon any other divisions, subsidiaries, business units, and/or affiliated companies of the Receiving Party that receive access to information related to the Pilot Study. The Receiving Party will ensure that all of its Affiliates sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing confidentiality restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "by exercising at least the same degree of care used to restrict disclosure and use of the Receiving Party's own similar confidential information, but at least a reasonable degree of care" has been removed, weakening the standard of care required. Further, the deletion of "The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by its Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party" weakens the agreement by not specifying how electronic copies should be handled. Additionally, the phrase "at least as restrictive as those set forth in this Agreement" was removed, implying that the receiving party can set any confidentiality restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Receiving Party shall preserve Proprietary Information received from the Disclosing Party in confidence and shall refrain from disclosing such Propriety Information to its employees not involved in the Pilot Study, its consultants, representatives, or agents ( the "Affiliates") or to any third party without written authorization from the Disclosing Party. This Agreement shall be construed to bind and impose obligations upon any other divisions, subsidiaries, business units, and/or affiliated companies of the Receiving Party that receive access to information related to the Pilot Study. The Receiving Party will ensure that all of its Affiliates sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement containing confidentiality restrictions.
perturbation.justification: The omission of "by exercising at least the same degree of care used to restrict disclosure and use of the Receiving Party's own similar confidential information, but at least a reasonable degree of care" weakens the standard of care in the Changed Text compared to what the original text implied. Furthermore, removing the specific requirements for electronic data deletion and the explicit statement that the NDAs signed by Affiliates must be 'at least as restrictive as those set forth in this Agreement' creates a contradiction. The Contradicted Text remains identical to the Changed Text but the change itself reduces the stringency of obligations regarding confidential information, potentially making it difficult to enforce the expected level of confidentiality, and causes an in-text contradiction as the current version of the agreement weakens the obligation set on the Receiving Party compared to the Original Text.

perturbation.original_text: 5. This Agreement shall not restrict disclosure or use of Proprietary Information that is: A. Now, or hereafter becomes, through no act or failure to act on the part of Receiving Party, generally known or available; or B. Known by the Receiving Party at the time of receiving such information as evidenced by records of the Receiving Party; or C. Hereafter furnished to the Receiving Party, as a matter of right and without restriction on disclosure, by a third party who had not received any Proprietary Information from the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party; or D. is approved by the disclosing party for disclosure without restriction in a written document which is signed by a duly authorized officer of such disclosing party; or E. Independently developed by the Receiving Party by persons who did not have access to the Disclosing Party's information and without breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. This Agreement shall not restrict disclosure or use of information related to the Pilot Study that is: A. Now, or hereafter becomes, through no act or failure to act on the part of Receiving Party, generally known or available; or B. Known by the Receiving Party at the time of receiving such information as evidenced by records of the Receiving Party; or C. Hereafter furnished to the Receiving Party, as a matter of right and without restriction on disclosure, by a third party who had not received any information from the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party; or D. is approved by the disclosing party for disclosure without restriction in a written document which is signed by a duly authorized officer of such disclosing party; or E. Independently developed by the Receiving Party by persons who did not have access to the Disclosing Party's information and without breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 5, the phrase 'Proprietary Information' is replaced with 'information related to the Pilot Study'. The contract is now contradictory because section 5's language contradicts the agreement's intent to safeguard explicitly specified proprietary information (algorithms, code, etc.) mentioned in the original definition in section 2. Since the changed section references a loosely defined 'information related to the Pilot Study', it creates uncertainty about what is truly protected from disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. This Agreement shall not restrict disclosure or use of information related to the Pilot Study that is: A. Now, or hereafter becomes, through no act or failure to act on the part of Receiving Party, generally known or available; or B. Known by the Receiving Party at the time of receiving such information as evidenced by records of the Receiving Party; or C. Hereafter furnished to the Receiving Party, as a matter of right and without restriction on disclosure, by a third party who had not received any information from the Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party; or D. is approved by the disclosing party for disclosure without restriction in a written document which is signed by a duly authorized officer of such disclosing party; or E. Independently developed by the Receiving Party by persons who did not have access to the Disclosing Party's information and without breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Proprietary Information' whereas the changed text replaces that phrase with 'information related to the Pilot Study'. This creates a contradiction because it broadens the scope of what is protected under the agreement. The change contradicts the agreement's intent to safeguard explicitly specified proprietary information (algorithms, code, etc.) mentioned in the original definition in section 2. Since the changed section references a loosely defined 'information related to the Pilot Study', it creates uncertainty about what is truly protected from disclosure. This difference creates a clear contradiction and uncertainty about the scope of protected information.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Upon termination, the Receiving Party shall cease use of the Disclosing Party's Proprietary Information, and shall destroy all Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, , together with all copies thereof, in its possession or control and furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction. Alternatively, at the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all such Proprietary Information, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the Proprietary Information, together with all copies thereof to the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by Receiving Party's Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Upon termination, the Receiving Party shall cease use of information related to the Pilot Study, and shall destroy all information related to the Pilot Study, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the information related to the Pilot Study, together with all copies thereof, in its possession or control and furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction. Alternatively, at the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all such information related to the Pilot Study, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the information related to the Pilot Study, together with all copies thereof to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By substituting 'Proprietary Information' with the looser 'information related to the Pilot Study,' the requirement to destroy all copies is weakened and becomes ambiguous. Furthermore, by deleting "The Receiving Party shall ensure that all copies of Proprietary Information stored electronically on its server, tape backup or on the hard drives of computers used by Receiving Party's Affiliates in connection with the Pilot Study have been permanently deleted and Receiving Party shall certify such deletion in writing to the Disclosing Party.", it makes it so the receiving party doesn't need to handle any information from the Affiliates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Upon termination, the Receiving Party shall cease use of information related to the Pilot Study, and shall destroy all information related to the Pilot Study, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the information related to the Pilot Study, together with all copies thereof, in its possession or control and furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction. Alternatively, at the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return all such information related to the Pilot Study, including without limitation, reports, analyses, e-mails or any other derivative embodiments of the information related to the Pilot Study, together with all copies thereof to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, by substituting 'Proprietary Information' with 'information related to the Pilot Study' and removing the specific requirement for the Receiving Party to ensure deletion of Proprietary Information stored electronically on servers, tape backups, or hard drives of computers used by Receiving Party's Affiliates, creates a contradiction. While both texts are the same, the change from the original to the changed text introduces the contradiction, as the original text specifically defines the scope of information that needs to be destroyed and includes affiliates while the changed text does not. This omission weakens the obligation and introduces ambiguity, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it relates to the original text and how it may impact other sections that pertain to what needs to be destroyed. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 15. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties, superseding all prior or contemporaneous communications, agreements and understandings between the parties with respect to the exchange of Proprietary Information in connection with the Pilot Study.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties, superseding all prior or contemporaneous communications, agreements and understandings between the parties with respect to the exchange of information in connection with the Pilot Study.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Proprietary Information' to 'information', there is an in-text contradiction introduced, given that the original definition of the original term Proprietary Information, listed a lot of things that now, if something is exchanged it is not longer considered as proprietary information and only as information with no further and specific protections
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties, superseding all prior or contemporaneous communications, agreements and understandings between the parties with respect to the exchange of information in connection with the Pilot Study.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Proprietary Information' to 'information' introduces a strong contradiction. The original text refers to 'Proprietary Information,' which, as explained, has specific protections. The changed text broadens the scope to simply 'information,' removing the explicit proprietary protections. This contradicts the implied understanding that information exchanged during the pilot study would be treated with the defined proprietary safeguards, creating a direct conflict in obligations and potentially rendering the protection of that information uncertain. Therefore, it is a clear contradiction.
